reforms.description: The Norwegian government has committed to rechannel spending of petroleum revenues towards measures that stimulate growth and production, including stronger prioritization of infrastructure investments and investments in health education and research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Redirecting petroleum revenues towards infrastructure and research is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has signaled its commitment to these priorities in the amended budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in fiscal policy, aiming to enhance long-term growth through strategic investments in key areas.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to rechannel spending of petroleum revenues towards measures that stimulate growth and production.

reforms.description: The Norwegian government has announced an increase in the ceiling on R&D support through the Skattefunn tax credit scheme, which is designed to incentivize innovation and R&D investment in the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing financial support for R&D, the reform aims to stimulate private sector innovation, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The ceiling on R&D spending eligible for the tax credit was substantially increased in the 2014 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing innovation support mechanisms, enhancing the capacity for R&D investment in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The ceiling on R&D spending that is eligible for the tax credit was substantially increased in the 2014 budget.

reforms.description: Norway is encouraged to ensure that entrepreneurial skills such as risk assessment, people management, project planning, and finance are included in the national skills strategy to foster innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing entrepreneurial skills is expected to improve innovation capacity and economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to include entrepreneurial skills in the national skills strategy has been made.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical gap in the skills strategy that could significantly impact innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: Ensure that entrepreneurial skills such as risk assessment, people management, project planning and finance are also given a place in the national skills strategy.

reforms.description: Norway is advised to encourage universities to further develop Technology Transfer Offices (TTOs) to enhance collaboration between universities and the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening TTOs is expected to facilitate the commercialization of research and enhance university-industry partnerships.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to develop TTOs has been made to enhance university collaboration.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to improve the transfer of knowledge from academia to industry, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Encourage universities to further develop Technology Transfer Offices (TTOs), including collaboration between universities.

reforms.description: Norway aims to continue supporting innovation through a competitive-bid based approach rather than automatic support, focusing on improving framework conditions and information flows for clusters.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a competitive environment for innovation, the policy is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to continue the competitive-bid based approach was made in the OECD Economic Survey.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how innovation support is allocated, moving towards a more competitive framework that could enhance the effectiveness of public funding.
reforms.source_quote: Continue to base innovation support on a competitive-bid based approach rather than automatic support.

reforms.description: Norway has implemented a specific strategy for Entrepreneurship in Education since 2004, aimed at promoting entrepreneurship awareness and skills among students.
reforms.package_name: Norwegian Action Plan for Entrepreneurship in Education
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering entrepreneurial skills and awareness, the initiative aims to enhance innovation and economic activity among future generations.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been renewed and continues to promote entrepreneurship in education.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to integrate entrepreneurship into education, which can have lasting impacts on innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A programme to promote entrepreneurship in education was first set up in 2004 and renewed in 2009.

reforms.description: The start-up grant scheme run through Innovation Norway received strengthened funding in 2013 to support new businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases access to finance for new ventures, facilitating their establishment and growth, which can enhance overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding for the start-up grant scheme was strengthened in 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strengthening of the start-up grant scheme represents a significant enhancement of support for new businesses, which is crucial for fostering entrepreneurship in Norway.
reforms.source_quote: The one public scheme that aims directly at finance for entrepreneurship is therefore focused on start-ups through a start-up grant scheme, run through Innovation Norway, whose funding was strengthened in 2013.

reforms.description: The Arena and Norwegian Centres of Experience (NCE) programmes were established to facilitate effective development within geographical clusters.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promotes collaboration and innovation among firms within clusters, enhancing productivity and competitiveness in targeted sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: Public funds are available for projects that improve interaction between firms and other agencies in clusters.
reforms.importance_rationale: These programmes represent a significant effort to enhance cluster dynamics and innovation, which is vital for economic growth in a small country like Norway.
reforms.source_quote: Public policy towards clusters...concentrates not on developing clusters per se, but on facilitating effective development within clusters.

reforms.description: The Skattefunn tax credit scheme, introduced in 2002 and expanded in 2003, allows firms to claim tax credits for R&D expenditures, significantly increasing support for R&D activities across all firms in Norway.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D spending, the scheme enhances innovation capacity and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit scheme has been operational since its introduction and expanded in 2003.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Skattefunn scheme represents a significant structural change in R&D funding, providing substantial financial incentives for innovation across all sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The tax credits scheme Skattefunn is the biggest individual support programme for R&D.

reforms.description: The project-based innovation arena (BIA) is the largest support programme for R&D grants in Norway, focusing on competitive project selection to enhance R&D and innovation across various industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding competitive R&D projects, the BIA fosters innovation and collaboration between public and private sectors, enhancing overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The project-based innovation arena is currently operational and supports various R&D initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The BIA represents a significant funding mechanism for R&D, promoting innovation through competitive project selection.
reforms.source_quote: The project-based innovation arena (BIA) is the biggest support programme for R&D grants.

reforms.description: Innovation Norway provides industrial support for private-sector innovation, particularly for start-ups, through grants, loans, and seed capital, although it has announced a reduction in such activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to funding for start-ups, Innovation Norway enhances the entrepreneurial ecosystem and promotes innovation-driven growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced a substantial reduction in such activities from 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the support provided is significant, the announced reduction indicates a shift in focus that may impact the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation Norway provides industrial support for issues not covered by the RCN’s mandate, including national and regional goals.
